segments.text: But, behold, they will not believe me, nor hearken unto my voice: for they will say, The LORD hath not appeared unto thee.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: What is that in thine hand?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: A rod.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Cast it on the ground.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And he cast it on the ground, and it became a serpent; and Moses fled from before it.
Put forth thine hand, and take it by the tail.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And he put forth his hand, and caught it, and it became a rod in his hand:
That they may believe that the LORD God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, hath appeared unto thee.
Put now thine hand into thy bosom.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And he put his hand into his bosom: and when he took it out, behold, his hand was leprous as snow.
Put thine hand into thy bosom again.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And he put his hand into his bosom again; and plucked it out of his bosom, and, behold, it was turned again as his other flesh.
And it shall come to pass, if they will not believe thee, neither hearken to the voice of the first sign, that they will believe the voice of the latter sign.

And it shall come to pass, if they will not believe also these two signs, neither hearken unto thy voice, that thou shalt take of the water of the river, and pour it upon the dry land: and the water which thou takest out of the river shall become blood upon the dry land.

footnotes: shall become: Heb. shall be and shall be

segments.text: O my Lord, I am not eloquent, neither heretofore, nor since thou hast spoken unto thy servant: but I am slow of speech, and of a slow tongue.
segments.speaker: Moses

footnotes: eloquent: Heb. a man of words
heretofore: Heb. since yesterday, nor since the third day
heading: Moses' Objection

segments.text: Who hath made man's mouth? or who maketh the dumb, or deaf, or the seeing, or the blind? have not I the LORD?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Now therefore go, and I will be with thy mouth, and teach thee what thou shalt say.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: O my Lord, send, I pray thee, by the hand of him whom thou wilt send.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Is not Aaron the Levite thy brother? I know that he can speak well. And also, behold, he cometh forth to meet thee: and when he seeth thee, he will be glad in his heart.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And thou shalt speak unto him, and put words in his mouth: and I will be with thy mouth, and with his mouth, and will teach you what ye shall do.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And he shall be thy spokesman unto the people: and he shall be, even he shall be to thee instead of a mouth, and thou shalt be to him instead of God.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And thou shalt take this rod in thine hand, wherewith thou shalt do signs.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Let me go, I pray thee, and return unto my brethren which are in Egypt, and see whether they be yet alive.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Go in peace.
segments.speaker: Jethro

segments.text: Go, return into Egypt: for all the men are dead which sought thy life.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And Moses took his wife and his sons, and set them upon an ass, and he returned to the land of Egypt: and Moses took the rod of God in his hand.
When thou goest to return into Egypt, see that thou do all those wonders before Pharaoh, which I have put in thine hand: but I will harden his heart, that he shall not let the people go.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Israel is my son, even my firstborn:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Let my son go, that he may serve me: and if thou refuse to let him go, behold, I will slay thy son, even thy firstborn.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And it came to pass by the way in the inn, that the LORD met him, and sought to kill him.
Then Zipporah took a sharp stone, and cut off the foreskin of her son, and cast it at his feet,
Surely a bloody husband art thou to me.
segments.speaker: Zipporah

segments.text: A bloody husband thou art, because of the circumcision.
segments.speaker: Zipporah

segments.text: Go into the wilderness to meet Moses.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And he went, and met him in the mount of God, and kissed him.
And Moses told Aaron all the words of the LORD who had sent him, and all the signs which he had commanded him.
And Moses and Aaron went and gathered together all the elders of the children of Israel:

heading: Moses and Aaron Before the Elders

segments.text: And Aaron spake all the words which the LORD had spoken unto Moses, and did the signs in the sight of the people.
And the people believed: and when they heard that the LORD had visited the children of Israel, and that he had looked upon their affliction, then they bowed their heads and worshipped.
